title_en: The Sixth Pillar: Belief in Divine Decree (Qadar)
content_en:
Of the six pillars of iman, belief in divine decree (qadar) is perhaps the most philosophically profound and the most prone to misunderstanding. Al-Uthaymin devotes a full chapter to it, carefully laying out what correct belief in qadar entails and correcting two historical deviations: the Jabriyyah, who denied human choice entirely and made people like compelled automatons, and the Qadariyyah (including the Mu'tazilah), who denied Allah's comprehensive knowledge and will over human actions.

The Sheikh organizes his explanation around four levels (maratib) of belief in qadar, which together constitute the correct Athari position:

The first level is al-ilm — affirming that Allah's knowledge encompasses all things from eternity, before their creation. He knew every event that would occur, every deed every person would perform, every word ever to be spoken. This is established by numerous Quranic verses and is a matter of rational necessity, since the All-Knowing cannot be unaware of anything.

The second level is al-kitabah — affirming that Allah recorded all that would happen in al-Lawh al-Mahfuz (the Preserved Tablet) fifty thousand years before the creation of the heavens and the earth, as established in the authentic hadith of Abdullah ibn Amr reported by Muslim. This is a matter of belief in the unseen that is accepted on the basis of revelation.

The third level is al-mashee'ah — affirming that nothing happens in the universe except by Allah's will. Whatever He wills comes to be; whatever He does not will does not come to be. This includes the actions of human beings, though they act through a real will and choice that Allah created in them. Al-Uthaymin is careful here to distinguish between Allah's universal will (al-iradah al-kawniyyah), which encompasses everything including disbelief and sin, and His legislative will (al-iradah ash-shar'iyyah), which consists of what He loves and is pleased with. Allah does not love sin even though He permits it to occur in the exercise of His wisdom.

The fourth level is al-khalq — affirming that Allah is the Creator of all things, including the actions of human beings. Their movements, choices, and deeds are created by Allah, though the human being is genuinely the performer (kasib) of his own deeds. Al-Uthaymin explains this without dissolving human responsibility: the Quran holds people accountable because they act with real will and choice, and divine foreknowledge does not compel their choices.

The Sheikh addresses the common objection: 'If everything is written, why should I strive?' He answers with the hadith in which the Prophet, peace be upon him, said: 'Strive, for everyone is facilitated toward that for which he was created.' Belief in qadar is meant to produce tranquility after one has exerted effort, not passivity before it. The great companions and the righteous were firm believers in qadar and were simultaneously among the most active and determined of people.

Finally, al-Uthaymin discusses the correct response to trials and difficulties in light of qadar: patience (sabr) during affliction, gratitude (shukr) during blessings, and repentance (tawbah) after sin — never blaming qadar as an excuse for one's own wrongdoing.
content_id:
Di antara keenam pilar iman, iman kepada takdir ilahi (qadar) mungkin yang paling mendalam secara filosofis dan paling rentan terhadap kesalahpahaman. Al-Utsaimin mendedikasikan satu bab penuh untuknya, memaparkan dengan cermat apa yang dimaksud oleh iman yang benar kepada qadar dan mengoreksi dua penyimpangan historis: Jabriyyah, yang sepenuhnya mengingkari kehendak bebas manusia dan menjadikan manusia seperti otomaton yang dipaksakan, dan Qadariyyah (termasuk Mu'tazilah), yang mengingkari pengetahuan komprehensif Allah dan kehendak-Nya atas tindakan-tindakan manusia.

Syaikh mengorganisir penjelasannya di sekitar empat tingkatan (maratib) iman kepada qadar, yang bersama-sama membentuk posisi Sunnah yang benar. Tingkat pertama adalah ilmu: iman bahwa Allah mengetahui semua hal yang terjadi dan akan terjadi, baik yang menyangkut perbuatan-Nya maupun perbuatan makhluk-Nya. Tingkat kedua adalah kitabah: iman bahwa Allah telah menuliskan semua hal ini dalam Lauh al-Mahfuzh (Lembaran yang Terjaga). Tingkat ketiga adalah masyi'ah: iman bahwa tidak ada sesuatu pun yang terjadi di langit maupun di bumi kecuali dengan kehendak Allah. Tingkat keempat adalah khalq: iman bahwa Allah adalah Pencipta segala sesuatu termasuk tindakan-tindakan manusia.

Salah satu pertanyaan paling halus dalam pembahasan takdir adalah hubungan antara kemahatahuan dan kehendak Allah di satu sisi, dan kehendak bebas manusia di sisi lain. Al-Utsaimin menjelaskan posisi Sunnah dengan hati-hati: manusia memiliki kehendak yang nyata dan kemampuan nyata yang mendasari tindakan mereka — mereka bukan robot — dan inilah yang menjadikan pertanggungjawaban moral masuk akal. Namun kehendak dan kemampuan ini sendiri adalah bagian dari takdir Allah dan terjadi dalam lingkup pengetahuan dan kehendak-Nya yang komprehensif.

Al-Utsaimin menekankan bahwa iman kepada qadar tidak boleh digunakan sebagai dalih untuk tidak bertindak atau untuk membenarkan dosa. Ini adalah kesalahan yang telah dikritik Al-Qur'an sendiri — mereka yang berkata "Jika Allah menghendaki, kami tidak akan menyembah selain Dia" (al-An'am: 148) sedang berargumen dengan takdir terhadap kewajiban-kewajiban agama, yang merupakan hujjah yang tidak valid. Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan para Sahabat (ra) untuk "Beramallah, karena setiap orang dimudahkan untuk apa yang ia diciptakan untuknya" — ini menunjukkan bahwa pengetahuan tentang takdir Allah tidak mengurangi kewajiban berusaha.

Buah-buah iman kepada qadar yang benar adalah ketenangan dan keteguhan. Seorang mukmin yang memahami bahwa semua yang terjadi berada dalam pengetahuan dan kehendak Allah tidak akan terlemparkan ke dalam keputusasaan oleh musibah atau kesombongan oleh keberhasilan. Al-Qur'an mengungkapkan ini secara indah: "Tiada suatu musibah pun yang menimpa di bumi dan tidak pula pada dirimu sendiri melainkan telah tertulis dalam kitab sebelum Kami menciptakannya. Sesungguhnya yang demikian itu adalah mudah bagi Allah." (Al-Hadid: 22)

Pemahaman yang seimbang tentang qadar — yang menegaskan pengetahuan dan kehendak Allah yang komprehensif sambil mengakui kehendak bebas dan tanggung jawab manusia yang nyata — adalah salah satu pencapaian teologi Islam yang paling halus. Al-Utsaimin menyajikannya dengan cara yang dapat dipahami oleh pelajar biasa, membuktikan kemampuannya sebagai pendidik yang telah menjadikannya salah satu ulama paling berpengaruh di abad kedua puluh.
content_ar: Of the six pillars of الإيمان, الاعتقاد in divine decree (القدر) is perhaps the most philosophically profound and the most prone to misunderstanding. Al-Uthaymin devotes a full الباب to it, carefully laying out what correct الاعتقاد in القدر entails and correcting two historical deviations: the Jabriyyah, who denied human choice entirely and made people like compelled automatons, and the Qadariyyah (بما في ذلك the المعتزلة), who denied الله's comprehensive العلم and will over human actions. The Sheikh organizes his explanation around four levels (maratib) of الاعتقاد in القدر, which together constitute the correct الأثري position: The أولاً level is al-ilm — affirming that الله's العلم encompasses all things from eternity, قبل their creation. He knew every event that would occur, every deed every person would perform, every word ever to be spoken. This is established by numerous القرآني الآيات and is a matter of rational necessity, بما أن the All-Knowing cannot be unaware of anything. The ثانياً level is al-kitabah — affirming that الله recorded all that would happen in al-Lawh al-Mahfuz (the Preserved Tablet) fifty thousand years قبل the creation of the heavens and the earth, as established in the authentic الحديث of Abdullah ibn Amr reported by مسلم. This is a matter of الاعتقاد in the unseen that is accepted on the basis of الوحي. The ثالثاً level is al-mashee'ah — affirming that nothing happens in the universe except by الله's will. Whatever He wills comes to be; whatever He does not will does not come to be. This includes the actions of human beings, though they act through a real will and choice that الله created in them. Al-Uthaymin is careful here to distinguish between الله's universal will (al-iradah al-kawniyyah), which encompasses everything بما في ذلك disbelief and الذنب, and His legislative will (al-iradah ash-shar'iyyah), which consists of what He loves and is pleased with. الله does not المحبة الذنب حتى وإن He permits it to occur in the exercise of His الحكمة. The رابعاً level is al-khalq — affirming that الله is the Creator of all things, بما في ذلك the actions of human beings. Their movements, choices, and deeds are created by الله, though the human being is genuinely the performer (kasib) of his own deeds. Al-Uthaymin explains this without dissolving human المسؤولية: the القرآن الكريم holds people accountable لأن they act with real will and choice, and divine foreknowledge does not compel their choices. The Sheikh addresses the common objection: 'إذا everything is written, why should I strive?' He answers with the الحديث in which النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, said: 'Strive, for everyone is facilitated toward that for which he was created.' الاعتقاد in القدر is meant to produce السكينة بعد one has exerted effort, not passivity قبل it. The great الصحابة and the الصالح were firm المؤمنون in القدر and were simultaneously among the most active and determine
